dimensions.note: Docs-only slice touching README and manifest description only; no .ts changed. No credential, token, or secret material introduced; the report remains SDK-free pure data shaping over workflow artifacts.
No .ts edited, so no logging changed. The change is limited to README prose and the manifest description.

No code touched. renderMarkdown still emits dedicated subsections for auth_expired, access_denied, and network, and counts other only in the summary tally; the never-throws guard is unchanged. The docs now match that behavior.

Docs-only change needs no new tests; the existing suite already covers the network subsection and the other-kind summary count, so the tests already back the corrected README/manifest wording. deno test for this extension stays green.
Report-only extension: renders collected workflow data and creates/updates/deletes no external resources, so idempotency does not apply. Unchanged by this docs slice.
No external API client; the report consumes swamp data artifacts only and validates each with safeParse before use. This slice edits documentation and does not alter any consumed contract.

No file handles, sockets, or temp files; docs-only change introduces no new resources. The corrected workflow YAML example was validated in a throwaway workflow (schema check passed) and removed, leaving no stray artifacts.

manifest version bumped 2026.06.26.1 -> 2026.07.19.0 (report has no model.version to align). README workflow example corrected to the real schema (jobs array, task block with modelType @jentz/aws-vpc-inventory + methodName scan, reports.require); coverage-gaps docs now include the network subsection and clarify that other-kind errors are a summary count with no dedicated subsection; manifest description aligned. No real account IDs, IPs, or hostnames in source, README, or fixtures.
